skill.name: stable-baselines3
skill.description: Guides Stable Baselines3 reinforcement learning work with algorithm selection, training templates, custom Gymnasium environments, callbacks, vectorized environments, evaluation, and model recording.
skill.summary: Build and evaluate Stable Baselines3 reinforcement learning agents with practical templates.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Manual review dismissed the static analyzer critical heuristic: the external command, weak crypto, C2, credential, reconnaissance, and network signals are documentation false positives. The real residual risk is moderate because the evaluation template loads model and normalization artifacts from user-provided paths, which should only be trusted local files. No evidence found of prompt injection, malicious intent, credential exfiltration, shell execution, or unauthorized network behavior.

medium_findings.title: Model and Normalization Artifact Deserialization
medium_findings.description: The evaluation template loads saved model files and VecNormalize statistics from caller-provided paths. This is expected for SB3 evaluation, but model and pickle-like statistics artifacts should be treated as trusted inputs only.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file paths are function parameters and the template calls SB3 load helpers directly. The behavior is legitimate, but unsafe if a user supplies untrusted model or statistics files.

low_findings.title: Template Writes Training Artifacts to Local Paths
low_findings.description: The scripts create log, model, and video directories and save trained artifacts. The paths are ordinary workflow parameters, so this is a low-risk filesystem behavior rather than malicious file access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem writes are explicit and limited to user-configured output directories for logs, checkpoints, and videos. No evidence of secret collection or hidden persistence was found.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The many external command, weak cryptography, C2 keyword, certificate, reconnaissance, and network findings are explained by markdown code fences, inline code, RL algorithm names, TensorBoard metric labels, and documentation links. No evidence found of shell execution, cryptographic operations, command-and-control behavior, credential harvesting, or unauthorized network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirmed the flagged lines are documentation or standard RL terminology. The only real code behavior found was local artifact handling in the Python templates.

dangerous_patterns.title: Model and Normalization Artifact Deserialization
dangerous_patterns.description: The evaluation template loads saved model files and VecNormalize statistics from caller-provided paths. This is expected for SB3 evaluation, but model and pickle-like statistics artifacts should be treated as trusted inputs only.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file paths are function parameters and the template calls SB3 load helpers directly. The behavior is legitimate, but unsafe if a user supplies untrusted model or statistics files.

content.value_statement: Reinforcement learning projects need correct algorithm choices, environment APIs, and evaluation workflows. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical SB3 guidance, templates, and review checklists.
actual_capabilities: Selects SB3 algorithms such as PPO, SAC, DQN, TD3, DDPG, A2C, HER, and RecurrentPPO for task constraints.
Provides training workflow guidance with callbacks, checkpointing, TensorBoard logging, and vectorized environments.
Explains how to build Gymnasium-compatible custom environments with valid spaces, reset, step, render, and validation methods.
Guides evaluation, model comparison, video recording, and VecNormalize statistics handling.
Documents callback patterns for early stopping, custom metrics, curriculum changes, and checkpoint behavior.
limitations: It does not run experiments, tune hyperparameters automatically, or guarantee trained agent performance.
It focuses on single-agent Stable Baselines3 workflows, not multi-agent reinforcement learning systems.
It assumes users can install and run Python, Gymnasium, PyTorch, and Stable Baselines3 dependencies.
Generated templates may need adaptation for production infrastructure, safety constraints, and custom simulators.

use_cases.title: Prototype a New RL Agent
use_cases.description: Choose an SB3 algorithm, create a training script, add evaluation callbacks, and save checkpoints for a first experiment.

use_cases.title: Create a Custom Gymnasium Environment
use_cases.description: Design observation spaces, action spaces, reward logic, reset behavior, and validation checks for SB3 compatibility.

use_cases.title: Evaluate and Compare Trained Agents
use_cases.description: Load trained agents, compare reward statistics, record videos, and handle normalization statistics during evaluation.

prompt_templates.title: Choose an Algorithm
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a Stable Baselines3 algorithm for an environment with [action space], [observation type], [simulation cost], and [training goal].

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Training Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an SB3 training plan for [environment name]. Include algorithm choice, vectorized environment settings, callbacks, checkpointing, and evaluation frequency.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Custom Environment
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Gymnasium environment design for SB3 compatibility. Check reset, step, spaces, reward logic, termination, truncation, info values, and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize an RL Experiment
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this SB3 experiment plan for instability, slow training, memory pressure, callback issues, vectorization problems, and evaluation bias.

output_examples.input: I need an SB3 algorithm for a continuous-control robot task with expensive simulations.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends SAC or TD3, explains the sample-efficiency tradeoff, suggests fewer parallel environments, and includes evaluation and checkpoint guidance.

output_examples.input: My custom Gymnasium environment fails SB3 validation.
output_examples.output: The skill checks space definitions, return signatures, data types, termination flags, image formats, and common observation-bound errors.

output_examples.input: I want to record videos and compare several trained policies.
output_examples.output: The skill outlines an evaluation workflow with deterministic actions, episode statistics, optional video recording, and normalization-statistics loading.

best_practices: Validate custom environments with SB3 environment checks before long training runs.
Use callbacks for evaluation, checkpointing, early stopping, and custom metrics instead of changing algorithm internals.
Save VecNormalize statistics separately and disable training updates during evaluation.
anti_patterns: Do not use DQN for continuous action spaces because it only supports discrete actions.
Do not evaluate normalized policies without loading the matching normalization statistics.
Do not start with SubprocVecEnv before debugging the environment with a simpler setup.

faq.question: Does this skill train agents for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and templates. You run the Python code in your own environment.

faq.question: Which Stable Baselines3 algorithms are covered?
faq.answer: It covers PPO, SAC, DQN, TD3, DDPG, A2C, HER, and RecurrentPPO guidance.

faq.question: Can it help with custom Gymnasium environments?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains required methods, spaces, validation, rendering, registration, and common environment mistakes.

faq.question: Does it support multi-agent reinforcement learning?
faq.answer: No. The guidance is focused on single-agent Stable Baselines3 workflows.

faq.question: Can it help evaluate saved models?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes evaluation, model comparison, video recording, and VecNormalize handling guidance.

faq.question: Should I load models from unknown sources?
faq.answer: No. Load model and statistics files only from trusted sources because deserialization can be unsafe.
